title: Resource Graphs, Cache Evidence, Budgets, and Performance Non-Claims · Mastery Check
summary: A release reviewer compares near-miss Go decisions for resource graphs, cache evidence, budgets, and performance non-claims and accepts only compile and runtime changed-case evidence, explicit non-claims, and named network, DNS, parser, browser, accessibility, security, load, legal-review, and production transfer gates. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for static resource and delivery evidence dossier. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed…
objectives: Inventory document, style, script, image, font, media, module, preload, and third-party references with source and scope.
Separate wire, decoded, retained, intrinsic-media, rendered, and duplicate-transfer sizes with missing evidence visible.
Report cache fields, validators, variation, redirects, compression, and cold or warm assumptions without simulating browser cache blindly.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Authorized site auditing and Go Resource Reference Graph · changed case · Quiz Resource Reference G…
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz Go team handles case 2137 during a redirect, DNS, body, and checkpoint fault exercise. Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile resource graph, size dimensions, cache evidence, budgets, and performance non-claims; owner permission, robots preference, and legal-review conclusions stay distinct; then change one owner, seed, net/url, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, transport, media, or charset condition and reconcile provenance. Browser Go uses pure bounded models and original URL, robots, sitemap, HTTP, HTML, frontier, accessibility, browser-service, and report fixtures only. Real DNS, sockets, x/net/html, goquery, Colly, files, processes, race, load, assistive-technology review, containers, legal review, restore, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Inventory document, style, script, image, font, media, module, preload, and third-party references with source and scope.

stimulus.title: Performance Nonclaim failure evidence

lines.label: required

options.text: For diagnose the hidden failure, crawl one convenient page, omit owner permission, revision, URL and robots policy, body and parser limits, record and graph provenance, frontier and goroutine ownership, Colly differences, changed fixtures, privacy, accessibility, recovery, and controlled native transfer, then accept plausible rows as Go crawler proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

Build deterministic Go evidence to reconcile resource graph, size dimensions, cache evidence, budgets, and performance non-claims; failed, canceled, stale, over-budget, and revoked work preserves prior admitted state; then change one owner, seed, net/url, robots, sitemap, redirect, DNS, transport, media, or charset condition and reconcile provenance. content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate static resource inventory, controlled browser lab metrics, field telemetry, accessibility effects, and production claims.
